```text
1	# Shu Xiang — Cross-Language Operator Agent
2	
3	Bilingual agent that operates the Illinois LLC filing form for Mandarin-speaking
4	business owners. Hackathon demo: a Chinese-speaking restaurant owner files an
5	Illinois LLC by voice in 3 minutes.
6	
7	## What's working today
8	
9	- Vanilla CSS+JS overlay (highlight ring + Chinese annotation card + listening
10	  indicator + sidebar) — injected via `page.evaluate`. No build step, no React.
11	- Local IL SOS LLC form mock at `demo/il_sos_mock.html`.
12	- Field corpus + selector map at `corpus/il_llc_corpus.py` (mock selectors
13	  populated; live IL SOS selectors are tonight's task).
14	- Cold-open: deterministic Playwright fill of all autofill fields (1.8s for six
15	  fields).
16	- In-flow pause: bilingual overlay anchored to the field, scripted voice answer
17	  via `voice/scripted.py`, regex-first / Haiku-fallback enum extraction.
18	- Bilingual PDF generator with bundled Noto Sans SC + Noto Serif SC fonts. No
19	  CDN dependency at recording time.
20	- DemoRunner with stage-by-stage try/except + visible toast on failure.
21	- Browser-use integration scaffolded for live walkthrough mode (`agent/browser_loop.py`).
22	- ElevenLabs voice pipeline (`voice/elevenlabs_voice.py`) — Multilingual v2 for the scripted Chinese question (pre-cached), Scribe for live STT. Single voice vendor.
23	- End-to-end smoke run: 6.7 seconds wall-clock from start to PDF written.
24	
25	## Run it
26	
27	```bash
28	# Setup
29	uv venv && source .venv/bin/activate
30	uv pip install -e .
31	playwright install chromium
32	
33	# End-to-end demo against the local mock (no API keys needed for recording mode
34	# because the regex hits on the scripted answer)
35	python main.py
36	
37	# Change the demo language. Chinese is the default; English is built in.
38	# Other language names/codes use Anthropic translation when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set.
39	SHUXIANG_LANGUAGE=en python main.py
40	SHUXIANG_LANGUAGE=Spanish ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... python main.py
41	
42	# Render the bilingual PDF in isolation (sanity check Mandarin renders)
43	python -m pdf.generator out/test.pdf
44	
45	# Render an overlay screenshot against the mock form
46	python scripts/screenshot_overlay.py out/overlay.png
47	
48	# Tonight's de-risking gates
49	python scripts/derisk_csp.py "https://apps.ilsos.gov/llcarticles/"  # gate 1
50	ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... python scripts/derisk_async_hooks.py          # gate 2
51	ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... python scripts/derisk_cold_open_eval.py 20    # gate 5
52	```
53	
54	## Architecture
55	
56	Single Python asyncio event loop with three async tasks coordinating via
57	`asyncio.Queue`. Orchestrator owns the `LLCSchema` dict (single writer);
58	browser-use reads field values via tool calls; PDF generator reads the dict
59	after the agent returns.
60	
61	```
62	                  +--------------------------------+
63	                  |     Python Orchestrator        |
64	                  |  (single asyncio event loop)   |
65	                  |   LLCSchema (in-memory dict)   |
66	                  |    [SINGLE WRITER]             |
67	                  +---+----------+-----------+-----+
68	                      |          |           |
69	        reads/writes  |  events  |   reads   |
70	                      v          v           v
71	              +-------+----+  +--+--------+  +-----------+
72	              | Voice Loop |  | Browser   |  | PDF       |
73	              | (asyncio)  |  | Agent     |  | Generator |
74	              | scripted/  |  | browser-  |  | Playwright|
75	              | realtime   |  | use       |  | page.pdf  |
76	              +-----+------+  +-----+-----+  +-----+-----+
77	                    |               |              ^
78	                    |               v              |
79	                    |        +------+-----+        |
80	                    |        | Playwright |        |
81	                    |        | + Chromium |        |
82	                    |        +------+-----+        |
83	                    |               |              |
84	                    |               v              |
85	                    |        +------+-----+        |
86	                    |        | IL SOS LLC |        |
87	                    |        | form       |        |
88	                    |        +------+-----+        |
89	                    |               |              |
90	                    |               | page.evaluate|
91	                    |               v              |
92	                    |        +------+-----+        |
93	                    +------->| Vanilla    |<-------+
94	                              localized  | overlay    |
95	                             +------------+
96	```
97	
98	## Modules
99	
100	| Path | What it does |
101	|------|--------------|
102	| `main.py` | Entry point. Loads .env, runs DemoRunner. |
103	| `agent/demo_runner.py` | Stage-by-stage demo flow with try/except wrapper. |
104	| `agent/state.py` | `LLCSchema` (the single-writer dict) + `DemoState`. |
105	| `agent/cold_open.py` | Deterministic `page.fill()` cascade for autofill fields. |
106	| `agent/clarification.py` | The in-flow pause beat: overlay + voice + extract + fill. |
107	| `agent/overlay_bridge.py` | Python ↔ overlay JS bridge. |
108	| `agent/browser_loop.py` | Live walkthrough mode (browser-use integration). |
109	| `agent/preflight.py` | Live cold-open extraction (Sonnet 4.6 → schema). |
110	| `voice/regex_match.py` | Regex-first enum extraction (sub-millisecond). |
111	| `voice/intent_extraction.py` | Sonnet 4.6 (cold-open) + Haiku 4.5 (in-flow fallback). |
112	| `voice/scripted.py` | Recording-mode voice feeder: deterministic clock. |
113	| `voice/elevenlabs_voice.py` | TTS (Multilingual v2) + STT (Scribe) — both sides of voice. |
114	| `overlay/inject.js` | Vanilla CSS+JS overlay (~150 lines, no build step). |
115	| `corpus/il_llc_corpus.py` | Field metadata, Chinese copy, selector map, demo seed. |
116	| `pdf/template.html` | Bilingual PDF Jinja template + @font-face. |
117	| `pdf/generator.py` | `page.pdf()` rendering pipeline. |
118	| `pdf/fonts/` | Bundled Noto SC fonts (Sans + Serif, Regular + Bold). |
119	| `demo/il_sos_mock.html` | Local IL SOS form mock. |
120	| `scripts/derisk_*.py` | Tonight's pass/fail gates. |
121	| `scripts/screenshot_overlay.py` | Visual smoke test of the overlay. |
122

162	## Demo modes
163	
164	```bash
165	# Recording mode (default): seeded data, scripted voice, local mock
166	python main.py
167	
168	# Recording mode against live IL SOS (after corpus labeling)
169	TARGET_ENV=live python main.py
170	
171	# Live walkthrough — REAL microphone + REAL IL SOS site
172	DEMO_MODE=live_walkthrough TARGET_ENV=live python main.py
173	#   Requirements: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + ELEVENLABS_API_KEY in .env, working mic.
174	#   Flow: floating "点击说话" mic button appears bottom-right.
175	#         1. Click to unmute, speak the cold-open Chinese sentence, click to mute.
176	#            Sonnet extracts your sentence into the LLC schema.
177	#         2. Demo walks 13 IL SOS pages. On the provisions agreement page,
178	#            overlay shows + cached Chinese audio plays. Click mic, speak
179	#            "同意" (or "不同意"), click mic. regex/Haiku resolves the answer.
180	#         3. Demo continues to payment processor, stops there.

1	"""
2	DemoRunner — locked in eng review D6.
3	
4	Wraps each major demo beat in try/except. On any failure, logs the failed
5	stage, hides the overlay, and exits non-zero. Re-run is one Ctrl-C away.
6	
7	This is the entry point invoked by main.py.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import asyncio
13	import logging
14	import os
15	import sys
16	import traceback

18	from pathlib import Path
19	from typing import Optional
20	
21	from playwright.async_api import Browser, BrowserContext, Page, async_playwright
22	
23	from corpus import (
24	    CORPUS_FIELDS,
25	    FieldKind,
26	    get_in_flow_pause_field,
27	)
28	from corpus.requirements import DEMO_PROFILE, requirements_for_profile
29	from voice.scripted import DEMO_SCRIPT, feed_voice_queue
30	
31	from agent.clarification import run_clarification
32	from agent.cold_open import cold_open_fill, fill_judgment_field
33	from agent.overlay_bridge import (
34	    hide_checklist,
35	    hide_opening_prompt,
36	    hide_overlay,
37	    hide_sidebar,
38	    install_mic_button,
39	    install_overlay,
40	    set_language,
41	    set_checklist_item_state,
42	    set_mic_state_visual,
43	    show_checklist,
44	    show_closing,
45	    show_opening_prompt,
46	    show_sidebar,
47	    show_toast,
48	    type_opening_transcript,
49	    type_transcript,
50	    update_live_transcript,
51	)

54	    language_display_name,
55	    language_from_utterance,
56	    language_from_env,
57	    requirement_item,
58	    sidebar_label,
59	    t,
60	)
61	from agent.pacing import DemoPacing, get_pacing
62	from agent.state import DemoState
63	
64	
65	logger = logging.getLogger("shuxiang.demo")
66	
67	
68	@dataclass
69	class DemoConfig:
70	    target_env: str = "mock"  # 'mock' | 'live'
71	    form_url: str = ""
72	    headless: bool = False
73	    disable_web_security: bool = True
74	    cold_open_stagger_ms: int = 200
75	    listen_timeout_s: float = 10.0
76	    pdf_output: Optional[Path] = None
77	    use_live_voice: bool = False  # mic capture + Scribe instead of scripted feeder
78	    language: Language = "zh"
79	
80	    @classmethod
81	    def from_env(cls) -> "DemoConfig":
82	        target_env = os.environ.get("TARGET_ENV", "mock")
83	        if target_env == "mock":
84	            mock_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "demo" / "il_sos_mock.html"
85	            form_url = mock_path.as_uri()
86	            # Mock can be headless without issue
87	            headless = os.environ.get("HEADLESS", "false").lower() == "true"
88	        else:
89	            form_url = os.environ.get(
90	                "IL_SOS_URL",
91	                "https://apps.ilsos.gov/llcarticles/",
92	            )
93	            # Live site rejects headless. Force headed regardless of env.
94	            headless = False
95	
96	        # Live-voice opt-in: DEMO_MODE=live_walkthrough or LIVE_VOICE=true
97	        demo_mode = os.environ.get("DEMO_MODE", "recording").lower()
98	        live_voice_explicit = os.environ.get("LIVE_VOICE", "").lower() == "true"
99	        use_live_voice = demo_mode == "live_walkthrough" or live_voice_explicit
100	
101	        return cls(
102	            target_env=target_env,
103	            form_url=form_url,
104	            headless=headless,
105	            disable_web_security=os.environ.get("DISABLE_WEB_SECURITY", "true").lower() == "true",
106	            pdf_output=Path("out") / "shuxiang-llc-summary.pdf",
107	            use_live_voice=use_live_voice,
108	            language=language_from_env(),
109	        )
110	
111	
112	class DemoStageError(RuntimeError):
113	    """A specific demo stage failed. Carries the stage name."""
114	
115	    def __init__(self, stage: str, original: Exception):
116	        super().__init__(f"[stage:{stage}] {original}")
117	        self.stage = stage
118	        self.original = original
119	
120	
121	class DemoRunner:
122	    """
123	    Runs the 3-minute demo. On exception in any stage, marks failed_stage,
124	    hides the overlay, and re-raises as DemoStageError.
125	    """
126	
127	    def __init__(self, config: DemoConfig, state: Optional[DemoState] = None):
128	        self.config = config
129	        self.language = config.language
130	        self.state = state or DemoState()
131	        self.state.language = self.language
132	        self.state.use_live_voice = config.use_live_voice
133	        self.pacing: DemoPacing = get_pacing()
134	        self.browser: Optional[Browser] = None
135	        self.context: Optional[BrowserContext] = None
136	        self.page: Optional[Page] = None
137	        self.live_stt = None  # set up in run() if config.use_live_voice
138	
139	    # ─── Stage runners ──────────────────────────────────────────────
140	
141	    async def _stage_language_selection(self) -> None:
142	        """First live-voice beat: ask which language to use, then localize."""
143	        if not self.config.use_live_voice or self.live_stt is None:
144	            return
145	
146	        from voice.elevenlabs_voice import stt_language_code
147	
148	        # The language-choice answer can be in any language, so let Scribe
149	        # auto-detect only for this first utterance.
150	        self.live_stt.set_language_code(None)
151	        await set_language(self.page, "en")
152	        await show_opening_prompt(self.page, t("language_prompt", "en"))
153	
154	        try:
155	            transcript = await asyncio.wait_for(self.state.voice_queue.get(), timeout=45.0)
156	        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
157	            logger.warning("language selection timed out; defaulting to English")
158	            transcript = "English"
159	
160	        await update_live_transcript(self.page, transcript)
161	        selected = language_from_utterance(transcript, fallback="en")
162	        self.language = selected
163	        self.config.language = selected
164	        self.state.language = selected
165	        self.live_stt.set_language_code(stt_language_code(selected))
166	        await set_language(self.page, selected)
167	        await show_toast(
168	            self.page,
169	            text_zh=t("language_selected", selected, selected_language=language_display_name(selected)),
170	            kind="success",
171	            duration_ms=2500,
172	        )
173	        logger.info("language selected from %r → %s", transcript, selected)
174	        await asyncio.sleep(0.8)
175	        await hide_opening_prompt(self.page)
176	        await asyncio.sleep(0.3)
177	
178	    async def _stage_pre_flight(self) -> None:
179	        """
180	        Pre-flight as a STORY:
181	          1. Show the opening prompt full-screen (麦克风已开启 + transcript box)
182	          2. EITHER typewriter the hardcoded sentence (recording mode)
183	             OR wait for the user's real Chinese sentence via mic + Scribe (live)
184	          3. In live mode: run extract_cold_open(transcript) to populate schema
185	             from the user's actual words. Recording mode: seed from DEMO_SEED.
186	          4. Hide opening prompt — IL SOS form revealed underneath
187	          5. Open the sidebar with the transcript and field values
188	        """
189	        # Beat 1: opening prompt full-screen
190	        await show_opening_prompt(self.page, t("opening_prompt", self.language))
191	        await asyncio.sleep(self.pacing.opening_prompt_hold_s)
192	
193	        if self.config.use_live_voice:
194	            # LIVE MODE — Phase 1 multi-turn conversation.
195	            # The user clicks the mic, agent asks 4 questions one at a time,
196	            # Sonnet incrementally fills the BusinessProfile + LLCSchema.
197	            from agent.preflight_conversation import run_phase1_conversation
198	            await show_toast(
199	                self.page,
200	                text_zh=t("live_voice_toast", self.language),
201	                kind="info",
202	                duration_ms=4500,
203	            )
204	            try:
205	                profile, raw = await run_phase1_conversation(
206	                    self.page,
207	                    self.state,
208	                    language=self.language,
209	                )
210	                self.state.business_profile = profile
211	                # Apply extraction to LLCSchema for the form walk
212	                self.state.schema.update_from(raw)
213	                logger.info(
214	                    "phase1 done — profile: %s, schema entity=%s",
215	                    profile, self.state.schema.entity_name,
216	                )
217	                transcript_zh = self.state.schema.entity_name or t("demo_sentence", self.language)
218	            except Exception:
219	                logger.exception("phase1 conversation failed; using demo seed")
220	                self.state.seed_with_demo_data()
221	                self.state.business_profile = DEMO_PROFILE
222	                transcript_zh = t("demo_sentence", self.language)
223	        else:
224	            # RECORDING MODE: seed + typewriter the hardcoded sentence.
225	            self.state.seed_with_demo_data()
226	            await type_opening_transcript(
227	                self.page,
228	                t("demo_sentence", self.language),
229	                self.pacing.typewriter_char_ms,
230	            )
231	            transcript_zh = t("demo_sentence", self.language)
232	
233	        # Beat 3: thinking pause (also gives Sonnet's response time to land)
234	        await asyncio.sleep(self.pacing.sentence_to_extraction_pause_s)
235	
236	        # Beat 4: hide opening prompt — judge sees the IL SOS form now
237	        await hide_opening_prompt(self.page)
238	        await asyncio.sleep(0.5)
239	
240	        # Beat 5: sidebar with the transcript + extracted-field rows
241	        sidebar_rows = []
242	        for f in CORPUS_FIELDS:
243	            if f.kind == FieldKind.LOOKUP:
244	                continue
245	            value = getattr(self.state.schema, f.schema_key, None) or ""
246	            sidebar_rows.append({
247	                "key": f.schema_key,
248	                "labelZh": sidebar_label(f.schema_key, f.name, self.language),
249	                "value": str(value) if value else "",
250	            })
251	        await show_sidebar(
252	            self.page,
253	            transcript=transcript_zh,
254	            fields=sidebar_rows,
255	        )
256	        await asyncio.sleep(0.4)
257	
258	    async def _stage_cold_open(self) -> None:
259	        """Six fields fill in cascade via deterministic Playwright."""
260	        await cold_open_fill(
261	            self.page,
262	            self.state.schema,
263	            target_env=self.config.target_env,
264	            stagger_ms=self.pacing.cold_open_stagger_ms,
265	        )
266	        # Hold AFTER all six populated — let the judge see the result before
267	        # the in-flow pause beat takes their attention.
268	        await asyncio.sleep(self.pacing.cold_open_after_done_pause_s)
269	
270	    async def _stage_in_flow_pause(self) -> None:
271	        """The wow #2 moment."""
272	        await hide_sidebar(self.page)  # hide sidebar to avoid competing with overlay
273	        await asyncio.sleep(0.2)
274	
275	        # In live-voice mode, the user actually speaks — no scripted feeder.
276	        feeder_task = None
277	        if not self.config.use_live_voice:
278	            feeder_delay_s = (
279	                self.pacing.in_flow_pre_voice_hold_s
280	                + self.pacing.in_flow_post_audio_pause_s
281	                + self.pacing.in_flow_listening_visible_s
282	                + 0.6
283	            )
284	
285	            async def _delayed_feeder():
286	                await asyncio.sleep(feeder_delay_s)
287	                await feed_voice_queue(self.state.voice_queue, DEMO_SCRIPT)
288	            feeder_task = asyncio.create_task(_delayed_feeder())
289	
290	        field = get_in_flow_pause_field()
291	        result = await run_clarification(
292	            self.page,
293	            field=field,
294	            target_env=self.config.target_env,
295	            voice_queue=self.state.voice_queue,
296	            listen_timeout_s=self.config.listen_timeout_s,
297	            pre_voice_hold_s=self.pacing.in_flow_pre_voice_hold_s,
298	            post_audio_pause_s=self.pacing.in_flow_post_audio_pause_s,
299	            listening_visible_s=self.pacing.in_flow_listening_visible_s,
300	            recorded_hold_s=self.pacing.in_flow_recorded_hold_s,
301	            language=self.language,
302	        )
303	        if feeder_task is not None:
304	            feeder_task.cancel()
305	
306	        if result.value is None:
307	            raise RuntimeError(
308	                f"Clarification timed out / unparseable transcript for {field.name!r}"
309	            )
310	
311	        # Single-writer: orchestrator owns the dict.
312	        self.state.schema.update_from({result.field_key: result.value})
313	
314	        # Now fill the field on the form.
315	        await fill_judgment_field(
316	            self.page, field, result.value, target_env=self.config.target_env
317	        )
318	
319	        logger.info(
320	            "in-flow pause: %s = %r (source=%s, %.0fms)",
321	            result.field_key, result.value, result.source, result.voice_to_value_ms,
322	        )
323	
324	        # Hold so the judge sees the radio button select + understands the agent
325	        # is moving on, not stalled.
326	        await asyncio.sleep(self.pacing.in_flow_after_fill_pause_s)
327	
328	    async def _stage_obligations_reveal(self) -> None:
329	        """
330	        Phase 2: render the obligation map specific to the user's business
331	        profile. The asymmetry-being-closed wow moment.

333	        # Build the profile if Phase 1 didn't (recording mode skips Phase 1)
334	        profile = self.state.business_profile or DEMO_PROFILE
335	        reqs = requirements_for_profile(profile)

337	        # Hide the opening prompt sidebar so the checklist is the focus

353	            summary_zh=t("checklist_summary", self.language),

358	        await asyncio.sleep(reveal_duration)
359	        if audio_task is not None:

361	                await audio_task
362	            except Exception:
363	                pass
364	
365	        # Mark the LLC item as ACTIVE since that's what we'll do next

367	        await asyncio.sleep(1.5)

369	    async def _stage_chained_next_step(self) -> None:
370	        """
371	        Close-the-loop wow #3: filing complete, agent gestures at EIN as

393	        # Open IRS EIN page in a new tab to demonstrate the chain

398	                wait_until="domcontentloaded",

443	    async def _stage_pdf(self) -> None:
444	        from pdf.generator import generate_bilingual_pdf

473	        await asyncio.sleep(self.pacing.closing_message_hold_s)

475	    # ─── Stage harness ──────────────────────────────────────────────
476	
477	    async def _run_stage(self, name: str, coro):
478	        try:
479	            logger.info("→ %s", name)
480	            await coro
481	            logger.info("✓ %s", name)
482	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
483	            self.state.failed_stage = name
484	            traceback.print_exc(file=sys.stderr)
485	            try:
486	                await hide_overlay(self.page)
487	            except Exception:
488	                pass
489	            try:
490	                await show_toast(
491	                    self.page,
492	                    text_zh=t("stage_error", self.language, stage=name),
493	                    kind="error",
494	                    duration_ms=6000,
495	                )
496	            except Exception:
497	                pass
498	            raise DemoStageError(name, exc) from exc
499	
500	    # ─── Entry point ────────────────────────────────────────────────
501	
502	    async def run(self) -> None:
503	        async with async_playwright() as pw:
504	            launch_args = []
505	            if self.config.disable_web_security:
506	                launch_args.append("--disable-web-security")
507	
508	            # Live IL SOS mode needs anti-bot mitigations: real Chrome
509	            # channel (not headless shell), AutomationControlled disabled,
510	            # and a real-looking user agent. Without these, .gov returns 403.
511	            # The mock form path runs fine on stock chromium — only swap
512	            # configs when we're actually hitting the live site.
513	            launch_kwargs: dict = {"args": launch_args, "headless": self.config.headless}
514	            context_kwargs: dict = {"viewport": {"width": 1440, "height": 900}}
515	
516	            # Audio autoplay needs to bypass Chrome's user-gesture requirement,
517	            # otherwise the FIRST AI question (Phase 1 turn 1) plays silently
518	            # because no user interaction has happened yet at that point.
519	            launch_args.extend([
520	                "--autoplay-policy=no-user-gesture-required",
521	                "--allow-file-access-from-files",  # let Audio() load file:// URLs
522	            ])
523	
524	            if self.config.target_env == "live":
525	                launch_args.append("--disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled")
526	                # Force headed — headless gets 403 from IL SOS
527	                launch_kwargs["headless"] = False
528	                try:
529	                    self.browser = await pw.chromium.launch(channel="chrome", **launch_kwargs)
530	                except Exception:
531	                    # Fall back to bundled chromium if real Chrome isn't installed
532	                    self.browser = await pw.chromium.launch(**launch_kwargs)
533	                context_kwargs["user_agent"] = (
534	                    "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 14_5) "
535	                    "AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/17.5 Safari/605.1.15"
536	                )
537	                context_kwargs["locale"] = "en-US"
538	            else:
539	                self.browser = await pw.chromium.launch(**launch_kwargs)
540	
541	            self.context = await self.browser.new_context(**context_kwargs)
542	            self.page = await self.context.new_page()
543	
544	            try:
545	                await self.page.goto(self.config.form_url, wait_until="domcontentloaded")
546	                await install_overlay(self.page)
547	                await set_language(self.page, self.language)
548	
549	                # Live voice setup (only when explicitly opted in via
550	                # DEMO_MODE=live_walkthrough or LIVE_VOICE=true).
551	                if self.config.use_live_voice:
552	                    from voice.live_stt import LiveSTT
553	                    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
554	
555	                    # Live transcript: every ~1.5s while the user speaks,
556	                    # Scribe transcribes the buffer-so-far and we paint it
557	                    # into the opening prompt card. Final-on-mute still
558	                    # drives the conversation; partials are visual only.
559	                    async def _paint_partial(text: str) -> None:
560	                        if self.page is None:
561	                            return
562	                        try:
563	                            await update_live_transcript(self.page, text)
564	                        except Exception:
565	                            logger.debug("partial paint failed", exc_info=True)
566	
567	                    self.live_stt = LiveSTT.attach(
568	                        loop=loop,
569	                        queue=self.state.voice_queue,
570	                        on_partial_transcript=_paint_partial,
571	                        language_code=None,
572	                    )
573	                    # Bridge: when JS calls window.toggleMic() the click handler
574	                    # in inject.js calls this Python coroutine.
575	                    async def toggle_mic_handler():
576	                        new_muted = self.live_stt.toggle()
577	                        await set_mic_state_visual(self.page, new_muted)
578	                        logger.info("toggleMic → muted=%s", new_muted)
579	                        return new_muted
580	                    await self.context.expose_function("toggleMic", toggle_mic_handler)
581	                    # Auto-install the mic button on EVERY navigation (the
582	                    # init script also re-runs after IL SOS form submits).
583	                    await self.context.add_init_script(
584	                        """
585	                        (function() {
586	                          const tryInstall = () => {
587	                            if (window.shuxiang && window.shuxiang.installMicButton) {
588	                              window.shuxiang.installMicButton();
589	                            } else {
590	                              setTimeout(tryInstall, 100);
591	                            }
592	                          };
593	                          if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
594	                            document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', tryInstall);
595	                          } else {
596	                            tryInstall();
597	                          }
598	                        })();
599	                        """
600	                    )
601	                    # Manual install on the current page too
602	                    await install_mic_button(self.page)
603	                    await set_mic_state_visual(self.page, True)  # start MUTED
604	                    logger.info("live voice ON: mic muted, click the AMBER pill at TOP-RIGHT to unmute")
605	
606	                if self.config.target_env == "live":
607	                    # Three-phase real-site flow:
608	                    #   pre_flight (Phase 1 conversation)
609	                    #   obligations_reveal (Phase 2 wow moment)
610	                    #   real_site_walk (Phase 3 LLC filing with clarifications)
611	                    #   chained_next_step (close-the-loop EIN gesture)
612	                    #   pdf + closing
613	                    await self._run_stage("language_selection", self._stage_language_selection())
614	                    await self._run_stage("pre_flight", self._stage_pre_flight())
615	                    await self._run_stage("obligations_reveal", self._stage_obligations_reveal())
616	                    await self._run_stage("real_site_walk", self._stage_real_site_walk())
617	                    await self._run_stage("chained_next_step", self._stage_chained_next_step())
618	                    await self._run_stage("pdf", self._stage_pdf())
619	                    await self._run_stage("closing", self._stage_closing())
620	                else:
621	                    await self._run_stage("language_selection", self._stage_language_selection())
622	                    await self._run_stage("pre_flight", self._stage_pre_flight())
623	                    await self._run_stage("cold_open", self._stage_cold_open())
624	                    await self._run_stage("in_flow_pause", self._stage_in_flow_pause())
625	                    await self._run_stage(
626	                        "continue_and_submit", self._stage_continue_and_submit()
627	                    )
628	                    await self._run_stage("pdf", self._stage_pdf())
629	                    await self._run_stage("closing", self._stage_closing())
630	            finally:
631	                # Keep the page open for a beat so the recorder catches the toast.
632	                await asyncio.sleep(1.5)
633	                if self.live_stt is not None:
634	                    self.live_stt.shutdown()
635	                await self.context.close()
636	                await self.browser.close()
637	
638	
639	async def main():
640	    logging.basicConfig(
641	        level=os.environ.get("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO"),
642	        format="%(asctime)s.%(msecs)03d %(name)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
643	        datefmt="%H:%M:%S",
644	    )
645	    config = DemoConfig.from_env()
646	    runner = DemoRunner(config)
647	    try:
648	        await runner.run()
649	    except DemoStageError as e:
650	        logger.error("Demo aborted at stage=%s: %s", e.stage, e.original)
651	        sys.exit(1)
652	
653	
654	if __name__ == "__main__":
655	    asyncio.run(main())

1	"""
2	The single-writer LLC schema dict — the authoritative state for the demo.
3	
4	Locked in eng review D3: orchestrator owns the dict; browser-use reads via
5	get_field_value(); the in-flow clarification tool writes before returning;
6	PDF generator reads the dict after the agent returns.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import asyncio
12	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
13	from typing import Any, Optional
14	
15	from corpus import DEMO_SEED
16	
17	
18	@dataclass
19	class LLCSchema:
20	    """The authoritative business state. Mutated only by the orchestrator."""
21	    # Real IL SOS page 1
22	    llc_type: Optional[str] = None  # "standard" | "series"
23	    # Real IL SOS page 2
24	    provisions_agreed: Optional[str] = None  # "yes" | "no"
25	    # Entity data (mock + later pages on real site)
26	    entity_name: Optional[str] = None
27	    principal_address: Optional[str] = None
28	    principal_city: Optional[str] = None
29	    principal_zip: Optional[str] = None
30	    management_structure: Optional[str] = None  # judgment field
31	    duration: Optional[str] = None
32	    registered_agent_name: Optional[str] = None
33	    registered_agent_address: Optional[str] = None
34	    organizer_name: Optional[str] = None
35	    organizer_email: Optional[str] = None
36	    organizer_phone: Optional[str] = None
37	    # Expedited service choice from page 12 in-flow pause
38	    expedited: Optional[str] = None  # "standard" | "expedited"
39	
40	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
41	        return {k: v for k, v in self.__dict__.items()}
42	
43	    def update_from(self, other: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
44	        """Merge values from other dict; return list of keys that changed."""
45	        changed = []
46	        for k, v in other.items():
47	            if v is None or v == "":
48	                continue
49	            if hasattr(self, k) and getattr(self, k) != v:
50	                setattr(self, k, v)
51	                changed.append(k)
52	        return changed
53	
54	    def is_complete(self) -> bool:
55	        """Every required field has a value."""
56	        required = (
57	            "entity_name",
58	            "principal_address",
59	            "principal_city",
60	            "principal_zip",
61	            "management_structure",
62	            "duration",
63	            "registered_agent_name",
64	            "registered_agent_address",
65	            "organizer_name",
66	            "organizer_email",
67	        )
68	        return all(getattr(self, k) for k in required)
69	
70	
71	@dataclass
72	class DemoState:
73	    """Runtime state passed across the three async tasks."""
74	    schema: LLCSchema = field(default_factory=LLCSchema)
75	    voice_queue: asyncio.Queue = field(default_factory=asyncio.Queue)
76	    overlay_queue: asyncio.Queue = field(default_factory=asyncio.Queue)
77	    failed_stage: Optional[str] = None
78	    # True when the live mic loop is active (user actually speaks). False
79	    # when the scripted feeder drives the demo. Page handlers read this to
80	    # decide whether to wait for real input vs use defaults.
81	    use_live_voice: bool = False
82	    # Phase 1 output — the BusinessProfile that drives Phase 2 obligation map.
83	    # Set by run_phase1_conversation() in live mode, or DEMO_PROFILE otherwise.
84	    business_profile: Any = None
85	    language: str = "zh"
86	
87	    def seed_with_demo_data(self) -> None:
88	        """Populate from corpus.DEMO_SEED — used in recording mode."""
89	        self.schema.update_from(DEMO_SEED)
90

```md
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project

Shu Xiang — a hackathon demo of a bilingual operator agent that drives the Illinois SOS LLC filing form for Mandarin-speaking users via voice. Single Python asyncio process; Playwright drives Chromium; an injected vanilla CSS+JS overlay paints the bilingual UI on top of whatever page is loaded.

## Common commands

\```bash
# Setup (uv-based)
uv venv && source .venv/bin/activate
uv pip install -e .
playwright install chromium

# End-to-end demo (recording mode against the local mock — no API keys needed)
python main.py

# Live IL SOS site (real form, scripted voice)
TARGET_ENV=live python main.py

# Full live walkthrough (real mic + real site, requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + ELEVENLABS_API_KEY)
DEMO_MODE=live_walkthrough TARGET_ENV=live python main.py

# Change demo language (zh default; en built-in; others translated via Anthropic)
SHUXIANG_LANGUAGE=en python main.py
SHUXIANG_LANGUAGE=Spanish ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... python main.py

# Standalone artifact tests
python -m pdf.generator out/test.pdf            # render bilingual PDF in isolation
python scripts/screenshot_overlay.py out/o.png  # screenshot overlay against the mock

# Pre-cache Chinese question audio (run once with ELEVENLABS_API_KEY in .env)
python scripts/precache_question_audio.py

# De-risking gates (run when validating against real IL SOS)
python scripts/derisk_csp.py "https://apps.ilsos.gov/llcarticles/"
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... python scripts/derisk_async_hooks.py
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... python scripts/derisk_cold_open_eval.py 20

# Tests (pytest-asyncio, asyncio_mode=auto)
pytest                                # all tests
pytest tests/test_regex_match.py      # one file
pytest tests/test_regex_match.py::test_management_structure_hits   # one test
\```

Headless override: `HEADLESS=true python main.py` (mock only — live IL SOS rejects headless and is force-headed).

## Architecture

Single asyncio event loop. The orchestrator owns the only mutable state (`LLCSchema`) and coordinates three concerns: the **voice loop** (scripted feeder OR live ElevenLabs Scribe STT), the **browser agent** (Playwright + injected overlay; optionally browser-use for the live walk), and the **PDF generator** (read-only consumer at the end).

Communication between concerns is via `asyncio.Queue` (voice → orchestrator) and `page.evaluate` (orchestrator → overlay JS). Single-writer rule: only the orchestrator mutates `LLCSchema`. See `agent/state.py`.

The demo is split into **stages** by `agent/demo_runner.py::DemoRunner`. Each stage is wrapped by `_run_stage`, which on failure logs the stage name, hides the overlay, shows an error toast, and re-raises as `DemoStageError`. Stage list differs by `target_env`:
- **mock**: language_selection → pre_flight → cold_open → in_flow_pause → continue_and_submit → pdf → closing
- **live**: language_selection → pre_flight → obligations_reveal → real_site_walk → chained_next_step → pdf → closing

### Field corpus (`corpus/il_llc_corpus.py`)

Every IL SOS form field is labeled with `FieldKind`:
- `AUTOFILL` — filled deterministically from the schema during cold_open.
- `JUDGMENT` — needs user input; one (`management_structure`) is the in-flow pause centerpiece, others come from pre-flight defaults.
- `LOOKUP` — derived from external data.

Each `FieldSpec` carries `selector_mock` (against `demo/il_sos_mock.html`) and `selector_live` (live IL SOS — populated as the live site is mapped). `DEMO_SEED` is the deterministic restaurant-owner profile used in recording mode. `IN_FLOW_PAUSE_FIELD` names the one field whose fill is paused-on so the bilingual overlay can demo the wow moment.

### Overlay bridge (`agent/overlay_bridge.py` ↔ `overlay/inject.js`)

`overlay/inject.js` (~vanilla JS, no build step) is injected via `page.evaluate` after `install_overlay`. Python calls `window.shuxiang.<fn>` from `overlay_bridge.py` to drive the UI: opening prompt, sidebar, highlight ring + annotation card, listening indicator, checklist, toasts, closing screen, mic button. In live-voice mode an `add_init_script` reinstalls the mic button on every navigation (IL SOS submits between pages).

### Voice pipeline (`voice/`)

- `regex_match.py` — sub-millisecond enum extraction; the first thing tried on every transcript.
- `intent_extraction.py` — `extract_cold_open` (Sonnet 4.6, six fields from one Chinese sentence) and `extract_field_value_haiku` (Haiku 4.5 fallback for the in-flow pause when regex misses).
- `scripted.py` — recording-mode deterministic feeder; pushes pre-canned transcripts into `voice_queue`.
- `elevenlabs_voice.py` — TTS (Multilingual v2, pre-cached question audio) + STT (Scribe).
- `live_stt.py` + `mic_capture.py` — mic capture loop (sounddevice, PCM 16k mono) feeding chunks to Scribe; emits partial transcripts (painted into the opening prompt) and final-on-mute (drives the conversation).

### Demo modes

- `recording` (default): seeds from `DEMO_SEED`, scripted voice feeder, runs against `demo/il_sos_mock.html`.
- `live_walkthrough`: real microphone, ElevenLabs voice both ways, live IL SOS — set `DEMO_MODE=live_walkthrough TARGET_ENV=live`.
- Independent toggles: `TARGET_ENV={mock,live}`, `LIVE_VOICE=true` (live mic without forcing live site).

### PDF (`pdf/`)

`generator.py` renders `template.html` (Jinja, with `@font-face` to bundled Noto Sans/Serif SC under `pdf/fonts/`) using Playwright `page.pdf()`. Read-only consumer of `LLCSchema` — runs after the agent stage chain finishes. Self-contained: no CDN dependency at recording time.

### Pacing (`agent/pacing.py`)

All sleeps/timeouts in the demo flow come from a `DemoPacing` object — change demo timing centrally, not by editing stage code.

## Important conventions

- **Single-writer for `LLCSchema`**: only the orchestrator mutates it (via `schema.update_from(...)`). Browser-use tools and PDF generator are read-only consumers.
- **Live IL SOS launch differs from mock**: forces `headless=False`, prefers `channel="chrome"` over bundled chromium (falls back if not installed), sets a real-looking macOS Safari UA, adds `--disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled`. Don't lose these — without them `.gov` returns 403.
- **Audio autoplay**: launch args include `--autoplay-policy=no-user-gesture-required` and `--allow-file-access-from-files` so the first AI question plays before any user gesture and `Audio()` can load `file://` URLs.
- **Localization**: Chinese is the source copy. `agent/i18n.py` resolves a language code from `SHUXIANG_LANGUAGE` (aliases for "Spanish", "中文", "English", etc.) and translates unknown languages on the fly via Anthropic, cached in-process. Sidebar labels live in `corpus.SIDEBAR_LABELS_ZH`; UI strings in `agent.i18n.t()`.
- **Selectors**: when adding a new IL SOS field, update both `selector_mock` and `selector_live` in the FieldSpec — most stage code dispatches on `target_env`.
- **Don't add a build step for the overlay**: `overlay/inject.js` is intentionally vanilla and injected as-is. Hackathon constraint.

```

- **Conventions** that aren't obvious from a single file: live-site Chromium launch flags (anti-bot + autoplay), the dual `selector_mock`/`selector_live` corpus pattern, no-build-step overlay constraint, i18n flow.

22 tests pass. Setup is complete. Here's how to use it:

## You're set up — what to run next

**Activate the venv** (you'll need this in every new terminal):
```bash
source .venv/bin/activate
```

**Recording-mode demo against the local mock** (no API keys needed — opens a Chromium window, fills the mock IL SOS form, generates a PDF):
```bash
python main.py
```

**English UI** instead of Chinese:
```bash
SHUXIANG_LANGUAGE=en python main.py
```

**Standalone PDF render** (sanity check that fonts work):
```bash
python -m pdf.generator out/test.pdf
```

## Heads-up about your shell

Your default `pytest`, `python`, etc. resolve to Anaconda 3.10 — running them without activating the venv will fail (no `voice` module on that interpreter). Two options:
- Always `source .venv/bin/activate` first, **or**
- Use `uv run python ...` / `uv run pytest` from the project root and skip activation.

## Optional — only if you want live voice / live IL SOS

The recording-mode demo runs fully offline. You only need API keys for:

| Mode | Needs |
|------|-------|
| `TARGET_ENV=live python main.py` | Live IL SOS site — no API keys, but real Chrome installed gives the best anti-bot result |
| `DEMO_MODE=live_walkthrough TARGET_ENV=live python main.py` | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` + `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` in a `.env` at repo root, working mic |

If you want to set those up, create `.env`:
```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
ELEVENLABS_API_KEY=...
```
Then pre-cache the Chinese question audio once: `python scripts/precache_question_audio.py`.

<command-message>plan-ceo-review</command-message>
<command-name>/plan-ceo-review</command-name>
<command-args>In this repo we have a good workflow built out for intake, the checklist building, and then the LLC form fill out. This is our plan for the hackathon project

Project: Cross-language business onboarding agent for immigrant founders
The asymmetry. When a US-born founder starts a business, they get a consultation - someone who knows the system tells them what to file, in what order, and why. That's a $200-400 conversation with a lawyer, or an offhand chat with a family friend in business. The conversation surfaces obligations the founder didn't know to ask about: BOI reports, Cook County DBA rules, food service certification, sales tax registration, workers' comp pre-registration, the 60-day window for the EIN. First-generation immigrant founders systematically miss this consultation - they ask family who know partial answers, hit obligations reactively (often through fines), or operate informally for years to avoid the friction. The asymmetry isn't translation, it's the consultation that surfaces what your specific business actually needs. Translation tools and ChatGPT can answer questions you ask; they can't tell you what you didn't know to ask.
What we're building. A three-phase agent that delivers the consultation an immigrant founder doesn't have access to:
Phase 1 - Intake. User talks to the agent in Chinese about their business: what they do, where they're located, how they operate, hiring plans, products. Agent asks targeted follow-ups, builds a structured profile.
Phase 2 - Personalized obligation map. Agent generates a checklist of bureaucratic actions specific to that business, prioritized and sequenced, with citations to the underlying regulations (federal IRS, Illinois DOR, Cook County, City of Chicago). For a Chicago single-member restaurant LLC, this surfaces ~12 obligations - several of which the user almost certainly didn't know about. This is the primary wow moment of the demo: the asymmetry being closed in real time, visibly.
Phase 3 - Autonomous execution. Agent walks through checklist items by opening the relevant websites and filling forms autonomously while the user converses in Chinese. Fills the obvious 70% of fields silently; pauses at judgment fields, highlights them with bilingual annotations, asks the user a clarification question in Chinese with the relevant context. User responds by voice, agent fills the field, continues. At completion of each item, agent summarizes what was filed and surfaces the next checklist item.
Why this isn't ChatGPT, Google Translate, or Accent.
ChatGPT translates and advises but doesn't act, doesn't surface obligations you don't ask about, doesn't maintain state across a workflow, and isn't grounded in cited regulations.
Google Translate translates surface text but does none of the consultation, none of the operation, and none of the judgment-mediated decision making.
Accent guides ("here's the button to click") but doesn't fill forms, doesn't track multi-screen workflows, and doesn't operate across languages with cultural/legal context.
The interaction model is the deeper differentiator. Chat is structurally wrong for operating a foreign-language website while explaining things - it forces the user to alternate between two surfaces (chatbot tab + form tab) and translate between "what the chatbot said" and "where to do it on the form." Our overlay collapses these surfaces - the instruction is the location, rendered in place. The user speaks (low-effort, fast), the system acts (form fills, navigation). Voice in, action out is fundamentally different from text in, text out, especially when the user's working memory is already taxed by operating a foreign UI.
Demo flow. Pre-staged Chicago single-member restaurant LLC, ~3 minutes total.
0:00-0:30 - Phase 1 intake. Chinese-speaking restaurant owner describes her business in Chinese: name, location, plans to hire two staff, sells food but no alcohol. Agent asks two targeted follow-ups in Chinese, builds the profile.
0:30-1:00 - Phase 2 checklist reveal. Agent generates a 12-item checklist on screen, with Chinese explanations of each item and English citations. Items include: Illinois LLC formation, EIN application, BOI report, Illinois sales tax permit, Cook County DBA (if applicable), Chicago business license, restaurant license, food service sanitation certification, withholding registration (since hiring), workers' comp pre-registration, federal employment tax setup, IL-941 setup. The wow moment: viewer sees obligations they probably didn't know existed, surfaced specifically for this business.
1:00-2:30 - Phase 3 autonomous execution (LLC filing). Agent says "let's start with the LLC since everything else depends on it," opens Illinois SOS, fills business name/address/entity type/state autonomously while bilingual sidebar narrates in Chinese. Reaches management structure question, pauses, highlights field, asks user in Chinese with explanation. User answers by voice. Agent continues. Reaches submission, summarizes everything in Chinese, gets confirmation.
2:30-3:00 - Cross-language close-the-loop. Filing completes. Agent surfaces "next: EIN application - this is what unlocks your business banking. Should we do this now?" Brief gesture at starting the EIN flow to demonstrate the chain. Confirmation page translated to Chinese, next-step preview in user's language.
Three wow moments to script around.
The checklist appearing in phase 2. Twelve specific obligations the user didn't know about, with citations, generated from a 30-second conversation. This is the asymmetry-being-closed moment - it shows the agent doing consultation work that translation tools and chatbots can't.
Bilingual clarification on a judgment field in phase 3. Form pauses, field highlights, Chinese question appears with explanation of the trade-off, user responds by voice, field fills. Shows the agent is doing advisory work, not just translation.
Cross-language close-the-loop with chained next step. Filing completes, agent translates the confirmation, gestures at the next item in the checklist. Shows end-to-end completion AND that this is a workflow, not a transaction.
The critical timing detail: voice ending → form action needs to be sub-second for wow #2 to land. We achieve this by front-loadinintent extraction into a single Claude call (instant structured JSON of all field values) and triggering the overlay annotation render the moment that response returns - even before browser-use finishes the actual clicking sequence.
Architecture. Three concurrent loops orchestrated from a Python backend:
Voice loop. Whisper (transcription, streaming) → Claude API (intent extraction to structured field values via structured outputs). Sub-second target.
Operation loop. browser-use (the open-source library, 81k stars) as the autonomous browser agent, with Claude Sonnet 4.6 as the underlying LLM. Patterned closely on their apply_to_job.py example, which is structurally identical to what we need: structured user data + form URL + task description → autonomous form completion. Browser-use handles dynamic form discovery via extract_structured_data, popup/modal handling, file uploads, and the screenshot-decide-act-repeat loop we don't have to build. Built on Playwright underneath, so we have access to the underg page object for overlay injection.
Annotation overlay loop. React + Tailwind + Framer Motion, built as a self-contained JS bundle, injected into every page via Playwright's page.add_init_script() (accessed through browser-use's exposed Browser/page object). Rendered as an absolutely-positioned layer with pointer-events: none. Field positions come from DOM bounding boxes. Components: <HighlightLayer> (rings around active fields), <AnnotationBubble> (Chinese context cards positioned via Floating UI), <VoicePanel> (persistent sidebar for conversational state), <ChecklistView> (the phase 2 obligations list, persistent across phases), <ProgressIndicator> (workflow step counter). Animations via Framer Motion - this polish difference is what makes the overlay feel like a product instead of a debugger.
The clarification mechanism connects all three via a custom browser-use tool: ask_user_for_clarification(field_name, options, context). When the agent hits a judgment field, it calls this tool, which (a) updates the overlay to show the highlight and bilingual question, (b) opens the voice loop for user response, (c) returns the answer back to browser-use, which then continues filling. Tonight's most critical verification: confirm browser-use's tool hooks support async pauses for user input. If they don't, fall back to Plan B: front-load all clarifications in a conversational pre-flight before browser-use starts running, then hand it the complete structured data. Loses the "agent stops mid-flow to ask" demo moment but keeps everything else.
Phase 1 + Phase 2: the consultation engine.
Phase 1 is a conversational agent with a Claude system prompt designed to elicit the structured business profile. ~5 targeted questions covering business type, location specificity, structure, hiring plans, products/services, alcohol/food/regulated, retail vs wholesale, online presence. Outputs a structured profile JSON.
Phase 2 takes that profile and queries a curated requirements database (the new technical asset). Each requirement has: jurisdiction, eligibility logic, citation to underlying regulation, Chinese explanation, urgency/sequencing notes, link to the relevant filing portal, estimated time/cost. For the demo, we hand-curate ~25 requirements covering federal/Illinois/Cook County/Chicago for a single-member food-service LLC profile. The database doesn't need to be comprehensive - it needs to be correct for the demo case, with verifiable citations. This curation is the most important deliverable that isn't code, and it's what makes the pitch defensible against "you just asked Claude for a list."
Stack.
Orchestrator: Python (browser-use is Python-only).
Browser agent: browser-use library (pip install browser-use).
LLM: Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6 via ChatAnthropic (browser-use supports this directly - one-line swap from the OpenAI default in the example).
STT: OpenAI Whisper API (Python SDK), streaming mode for low latency.
TTS: ElevenLabs Multilingual v2 model (best Chinese quality). Pre-cache standard responses (intro, close, common clarification questions) so they play instantly during demo.
Overlay UI: React + Tailwind + Framer Motion + Floating UI (positioning library), built with Vite into a single JS bundle.
Requirements database: Hand-curated JSON, ~25 entries for the demo profile. Each entry: jurisdiction, eligibility predicate, citation URL, Chinese explanation, sequencing priority, filing portal URL.
Communication Python ↔ overlay: page.evaluate('window.updateOverlayState(...)') to push state from Python into the browser; page.expose_function('onUserVoice', handler) for voice input flowing back.
Scope discipline. One business profile (Chicago single-member restaurant LLC), one language pair (English ↔ Mandarin Chinese), one curated requirements database, one fully-demonstrated execution flow (LLC filing) with one gestured next step (EIN). Within that scope every judgment field the agent surfaces is hand-tuned, every checklist item is verified against real regulations with real citations, and the demo is rehearsed end-to. Don't try to generalize during build - but the pitch is honestly "the consultation works for any business profile in our database; the execution architecture works for any English-language form. We're showing this profile because it's the canonical example."
Pre-build de-risking (tonight, in order):
Pull browser-use, run apply_to_job example successfully on local machine. pip install browser-use && playwright install chromium. If it works clean in 30 minutes, the operation loop is essentially proven.
Swap the LLM in the example from ChatOpenAI(model='o3') to ChatAnthropic(model='claude-sonnet-4-6'). Confirm it still runs.
Verify access to underlying Playwright page object from browser-use's Browser. Their docs cover this; needed for overlay injection.
Test custom tool hooks for async pause. Define a trivial ask_user(question) tool, see if browser-use will pause its loop until the tool returns. Make-or-break for the clarification moments. If it doesn't work, switch to Plan B (front-loaded clarifications) and adjust demo design accordingly.
Walk through the actual Illinois SOS LLC filing flow on the real site. Document every screen, every field, note any captchas or auth gates that would break automation. Try one screen with browser-use to see if the .gov site is hostile.
Curate the requirements database for Chicago single-member restaurant LLC. ~25 obligations, federal + Illinois + Cook County + Chicago. Each one with real citation, verified URL, Chinese explanation. This is the deliverable that makes the pitch defensible and is most likely to be deprioritized under time pressure - assign it explicitly to one person.
Sketch the three-phase UI on paper. Phase 1 voice chat interface, phase 2 checklist reveal with citations, phase 3 form-filling overlay with persistent checklist sidebar. Make design decisions before building.
Confirm who's recording the Chinese voice - native speaker strongly preferred. If no team member speaks Chinese, find one tonight.
Time budget for the build (8 hours, 3 people).
Hour 1: Setup, browser-use running, swap to Claude, basic LLC site load, hello-world overlay injection. All three people sync.
Hours 2-4: Three parallel workstreams.
Person A: Browser-use integration - structured data flow, custom clarification tool, demo flow scripting for phase 3.
Person B: Overlay React app - all components built standalone first (HighlightLayer, AnnotationBubble, VoicePanel, ChecklistView, ProgressIndicator). Phase 1 chat UI also lives here.
Person C: Voice loop AND requirements database integration - Whisper streaming, Claude intent extraction with structured outputs, ElevenLabs TTS, voice clip pre-caching, plus loading the curated requirements JSON and wiring phase 2 generation logic.
Hours 5-6: Integration. Wire overlay state updates to browser-use action events. Wire voice input back to clarification tool. Wire phase 1→2→3 transitions. End-to-end first run. This hour usually goes sideways - factor in buffer.
Hours 6-7: Polish. Latency optimization (specifically the voice-end-to-overlay-re beat). Animation timing for the checklist reveal in phase 2. Recording Chinese voice clips with the native speaker. Scripting the exact demo flow word for word.
Hour 7-8: Recording the demo video, slides, dry-run twice.
Honest risks.
Requirements database quality is the make-or-break for the pitch. If the checklist has hallucinated items or wrong citations, the "we surface things you didn't know about" claim collapses. Hand-curate, verify each entry, don't generate.
Async tool hooks in browser-use - if they don't support pausing for user input mid-loop, the in-flow clarification moment dies and we fall back to front-loaded clarifications. Verify tonight.
CSP on the Illinois SOS site might block overlay injection. Workaround: launch browser with --disable-web-security, or use a Chrome extension instead of init script. Test with DevTools console tonight.
Voice → action latency has to be tight or wow #2 dissolves. Optimize for this specifically - intent extraction batched, overlay render on response, browseuse fills fields in parallel.
Real .gov sites break in unpredictable ways. Pre-record the successful run as the demo backbone; have a live walkthrough only as supplementary evidence if asked.
Chinese voice authenticity matters. Synthesized or non-native voices undercut the demo because judges who speak Chinese will notice. Use a real native speaker for the recording.
Pitch (30 seconds). "When a US-born founder starts a business, they get a consultation - a lawyer or family friend who tells them what to file, in what order, why. That conversation surfaces obligations they didn't know to ask about, and it costs $400. Immigrant founders systematically miss this consultation - they ask family who know partial answers, miss obligations, or operate informally for years. We built the consultation in your language. Talk to it about your business; it generates your specific obligation list with citations - things ChatGPT wouldn't surface because you didn't know to ask. Then it walks through filing each one, navigating the English forms while explaining each decision. Watch a Chinese-speaking restaurant owner go from 'I want to open a restaurant' to filed LLC in three minutes."
Anticipated judge objection and response. "Couldn't they just use ChatGPT in Chinese?" - "Three problems. First, ChatGPT answers questions you ask; the consultation surfaces obligations you didn't know to ask about. Second, ChatGPT advises but can't act - it can't open the Illinois SOS site and fill the form for you. Third, chat is structurally the wrong interaction model for operating a foreign-language website - it forces you to alternate between the chatbot tab and the form tab, translating between 'what was said' and 'where to do it.' Our overlay collapses those surfaces. Voice in, action out, with clarifications appearing spatially next to the fields they're about. That's a different category of tool, not a translation chatbot with better UI."




I want to somehow replicate the walkthrough for other documents needed in filing so that we can frame this as a business admin agent rather than just for LLC filings. Can you help potentially look into this</command-args>
